Name: San Juan Town Park
Description: Park at Town Park. Fishing spots 01-03 all walkable along the river. Riverwalk hike starts here too.

Name: Mary Fisher Park
Description: Free lot. Across the river from Town Park. Bank access and shallow riffles.

Name: Yamaguchi Park
Description: South end of town. Quiet deep run. Walk-in from park lot.

Name: East Fork San Juan River
Description: Multiple pullouts off FR 667 north of town. Park at any pullout with river access.

Name: Piedra River Lower Canyon
Description: Multiple pullouts off CR 600 (Piedra Road) west of town. Park at any pullout for wade access.

Name: Williams Creek Trailhead
Description: Same lot serves Williams Creek fishing AND the Williams Creek Trail (to CDT). About 30 min north.

Name: Echo Canyon Reservoir
Description: Right off Hwy 84. Easy bank access lot. About 20 min southeast.

Name: Blanco River / Blanco Basin
Description: Blanco River Group Campground on the water. Park here for fishing AND the Blanco Basin hike.

Name: Navajo River
Description: Lightly pressured remote stream near NM border. About 40 min southwest.

Name: Piedra River Upper Trailhead
Description: One lot — three options: hike-in fishing (spot 10) + Piedra Family hike (3-4 mi) + Full Piedra River Trail (11 mi).

Name: Turkey Creek Trailhead
Description: Same trailhead for hike-in fishing and Turkey Creek Trail. Coords approximate — verify in Maps. About 40 min north.

Name: Fish Creek
Description: Remote creek east of town via forest roads. About 50 min east.

Name: San Juan River Texas Hole
Description: World-class tailwater below Navajo Dam. 1.5 hrs south in New Mexico. Large designated lot.

Name: Reservoir Hill Trailhead
Description: Access from Hot Springs Blvd or behind the San Juan Motel. Street parking. Right behind town.

Name: Coyote Hill Trailhead
Description: Off Piedra Road. Small pullout at trailhead. 2.5 mi loop with mountain views.

Name: Fourmile Falls Trailhead
Description: Take CR 400 north ~8 mi then Fourmile Road another 5 mi. Dedicated trailhead lot. Waterfall at peak flow in June.

Name: Cimarrona Creek Trailhead
Description: Off Williams Lake Road past Cimarrona Campground. Trailhead at 8400 ft. Lower sections clear in June.

Name: West Fork Trailhead
Description: Forest Service Road 648 past West Fork Campground. Serious stream crossings in early June runoff.
